_comment: Organ-specific weight change thresholds for regulatory toxicology assessment. Sources: FDA reviewer practice, Hood (2006), Hall (2012), Capen (1997), ECETOC TRA.

_schema.variation_ceiling_pct: Below this % change, the finding is trivially small (noise ceiling)
_schema.adverse_floor_pct: At or above this % change, the finding is biologically meaningful (adverse floor)
_schema.strong_adverse_pct: At or above this, the finding is clearly adverse regardless of context
_schema.adaptive_requires: Optional block: conditions required to claim 'adaptive' for this organ
_schema.special_flags: Optional array: organ-specific caveats the engine should annotate
_schema.cross_organ_link: Optional: mechanistic link to another organ's findings

LIVER.notes: Liver is the most studied organ for adaptive vs adverse distinction. Hall 2012 defined the LB panel gate.

KIDNEY.notes: Kidney weight changes ≥10% are concerning. Male rat α2u-globulin nephropathy is not human-relevant.

HEART.notes: Heart has low normal variation (CV ~5-8%). Even small changes are meaningful.

BRAIN.notes: Brain weight is tightly regulated. Any statistically significant change is biologically meaningful. Uses any_significant policy.

ADRENAL.notes: Adrenal weight is highly variable, especially in mice. Species-specific thresholds prevent false positives.

THYROID.notes: Thyroid weight changes often secondary to liver enzyme induction (liver-thyroid axis). Capen 1997.

SPLEEN.notes: Spleen weight is naturally variable. Large changes may indicate immune response or stress.

THYMUS.notes: Thymus weight is age-sensitive and stress-responsive. Involution is normal in older animals.

TESTES.notes: Testes weight is tightly regulated. Changes ≥10% are concerning for reproductive toxicity.

EPIDIDYMIDES.notes: Epididymal weight changes often parallel testicular effects.

OVARIES.notes: Ovarian weight is highly variable due to estrous cycle stage. Higher thresholds prevent false positives.

UTERUS.notes: Uterine weight is the most variable organ weight due to estrous cycle. Very high thresholds required.

LUNGS.notes: Lung weight changes may indicate edema, inflammation, or fibrosis. Residual blood can confound.
